title: quercus edwardsiae
intro: Quercus edwardsiae, also known as Edwards' oak or Edwards scrub oak, is a small to medium-sized evergreen tree or shrub native to California in the United States. It is a member of the white oak family, Quercaceae, and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Quercus edwardsiae include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of Quercus edwardsiae can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are often used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
